column3: TKIP was the transitional encryption method used with the original WPA on existing hardware.
column4: Associate TKIP with the older WPA transition. AES is the stronger encryption choice associated with WPA2.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.3; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 31
column9: A+::220-1202::2.3 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::WirelessSecurityProtocolsandAuthentication Cloze HighYield TKIP WirelessEncryption Acronyms Source::Messer-v140

column3: WPA2 uses AES, which provides stronger encryption than legacy TKIP.
column4: The acronym expansion is the recall target. The applied Basic cards separately test when to choose AES and how it relates to WPA2.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.3; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 31
column9: A+::220-1202::2.3 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::WirelessSecurityProtocolsandAuthentication Cloze HighYield AES WirelessEncryption Acronyms Source::Messer-v140

column2: RADIUS expands to {{c1::Remote Authentication Dial-in User Service}}.
column3: RADIUS provides centralized AAA for services such as wireless 802.1X and VPN access; it is not limited to dial-up connections.
column4: Do not let “Dial-in” make the term seem obsolete. The applied Basic card tests its modern centralized-authentication role.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.3; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 31
column9: A+::220-1202::2.3 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::WirelessSecurityProtocolsandAuthentication Cloze RADIUS AAA Acronyms Source::Messer-v140

column2: TACACS+ is the current “plus” version of {{c1::Terminal Access Controller Access-Control System}}.
column3: TACACS+ is a remote authentication protocol commonly associated with Cisco network devices.
column4: The plus sign distinguishes the current protocol described by the objective from earlier TACACS versions.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.3; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, pp. 31–32
